description: Returning parents are identified by email address match. If a parent uses a different email, they will be treated as a new lead.
Parent-facing enrollment and trial booking flows in MVP are public (no login required). Parents access them via links shared by admin or found on the website. Email verification confirms identity.
isolation_note: Keep parent identity resolution separate from flow logic so adding login in Phase 2 does not require rewriting enrollment forms

description: Facebook Lead Form integration uses a webhook or polling mechanism to auto-create lead records. Specific integration method deferred to implementation.
Google Forms/Ads lead capture uses a similar ingest mechanism as Facebook leads. All lead sources funnel into the same lead record format.
Calendar invites (e.g., .ics files or Google Calendar links) are optional in MVP and may be sent as attachments in confirmation emails.
Coupon codes are created and managed by admin. There is no self-serve coupon generation. Coupons have optional expiry dates and usage limits.
Payment overdue threshold is configurable by admin (e.g., 3 days, 7 days after enrollment). Default is 3 days.
Admin dashboard metrics are real-time (or near real-time) calculated from current data, not pre-aggregated reports.
The system supports a single location in MVP. Multi-location support is Phase 2 but the data model includes location_id from the start.
Staff role in Phase 2 has limited access: can view calendar and enrollment list but cannot modify programs, pricing, or payment records.
Trial booking requires selecting a specific date from admin-defined trial-eligible dates. Walk-in trials are tracked by admin manually updating lead status.
Enrollment capacity is per-program (not per-session). When a program reaches capacity, enrollment is locked for that program.
isolation_note: Capacity logic should be configurable (per-program vs per-session) to allow future flexibility without restructuring

description: Partial payments are tracked manually by admin. The system does not enforce payment installment schedules automatically.
COPPA may not apply because child data (name, age) is collected from parents/guardians, not directly from children. However, this requires legal/compliance confirmation before launch.
isolation_note: If COPPA applies, verifiable parental consent mechanisms and data handling policies must be added before collecting any child PII
